facet: placement churn / split-brain window — does reassigning a peer between relays converge without stranding, and what is the partition window?
topology: relay-A on node-1 (172.31.43.122:3343/q3478), relay-B on node-2 (172.31.19.13:3343/q3478). One mobile peer = a responder with a PINNED secret (stable endpoint id across restarts). Connector = e2-connect (relay-only, resolves the peer by bare id through the controller-ASSIGNED relay via MemoryLookup), run on node-1, knows both relays.
method: 1) home the peer on relay-A; connect with assign=A. 2) re-home the peer to relay-B (kill on node-1, restart on node-2 with the SAME secret => same id). 3) connect with the now-stale assign=A. 4) connect with assign=B. Record connected + relay_used per step.

steps.note: homed on A, resolves+connects via A
pinned secret => identical endpoint id after the move (the SAME peer moved, not a new one)
peer is now on B; the stale A assignment points at a relay it no longer homes, and there is NO relay-to-relay mesh => unreachable. This is the partition/split-brain window (~ TTL + reconnect).
updated placement resolves+connects via B — converged, peer reachable on its new relay

result.exit: MET — reassignment converges: after re-homing, the peer is reachable via its new relay (assign=B connects) and no endpoint is left unreachable once placement is updated. The churn window is real and bounded: while the assignment is stale (points at the old relay), the connection fails (no relay mesh) until the controller publishes the new relay. Confirms E2's co-location-is-mandatory finding under motion: placement must track the peer or the peer is unreachable for the window.
result.what_tolerates_the_window: Eventual-sync workloads tolerate it (they retry/reconcile once placement updates); live presence does not (a connection attempted mid-window fails). A straddling superpeer (meer) homed on BOTH relays during the move would close the window — the E8 fork.
result.caveat: The window here is measured as a binary fail (12s connect timeout) during the stale period, not a precise TTL-to-reconnect distribution; quantifying the window length vs DNS/pkarr TTL is the integration follow-on (E2's pending DNS-origin form). Single peer, single move — not a churn STORM (many peers reassigned concurrently), which would also stress the controller + relay accept rate.
